issues.title: TextEditingController Textspan's recognizer raising error on initializing text
issues.body:
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/58595897/151553482-d8c685c1-2681-4872-a743-e00932d1e829.mov


Here I extend TextEditingController to highlight(also clickable) the keyword "ali". It is working perfectly whenever the user types "ali". But when initializing text to the controller, it is showing a lot of errors. Someone pls help me with how I can resolve this error.

Note: When I initialize the text and remove "recognizer" from "textspan" it is working perfectly without showing any error. I guess this issue is happening because of "recognizer" in the textspan.

here is my code, pls uncomment line number 15 to see the error.

```dart
import 'package:flutter/gestures.dart';
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';
import 'package:kyubook/utils/utility.dart';

class TexspanTap extends StatefulWidget {
  @override
  _TexspanTapState createState() => _TexspanTapState();
}

class _TexspanTapState extends State<TexspanTap> {
  final MyTextEditingcontroller _editingcontroller = MyTextEditingcontroller();

  @override
  void initState() {
    //_editingcontroller.text = "dghali";
    super.initState();
  }

  @override
  void dispose() {
    _editingcontroller.dispose();
    super.dispose();
  }

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(),
      body: SafeArea(
        child: Column(
          children: [
            TextField(
              controller: _editingcontroller,
            ),
          ],
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

class MyTextEditingcontroller extends TextEditingController {
  @override
  TextSpan buildTextSpan(
      {required BuildContext context,
      TextStyle? style,
      required bool withComposing}) {
    if (text.toLowerCase().contains("ali")) {
      final List<InlineSpan> spans = [];

      final int indexOfAli = text.indexOf("ali");

      spans.add(TextSpan(text: text.substring(0, indexOfAli)));

      spans.add(
        TextSpan(
            text: text.substring(indexOfAli, indexOfAli + 3),
            style: const TextStyle(color: Colors.red),
            recognizer: TapGestureRecognizer()
              ..onTapDown = (final details) {
                commonToast("Touched");
              }),
      );

      spans.add(TextSpan(text: text.substring(indexOfAli + 3, text.length)));

      return TextSpan(children: spans, style: style);
    }

    return TextSpan(text: text, style: style);
  }
}
```

issues.title: Missing cursor and paddings for TextFormField on certain linux distros
comments:
Hi @TahaTesser,
thank you for feedback. I think you are right, it is a distro problem. I also tried this bug app in two other distros: Fedora 31 and Centos 7. Both have no issues. Unfortunately I have no more other distros. Currently, my different systems show following behavior (all three systems have Gnome, but different versions):
- Fedora 30: no cursor, text overlaps with counter, some additional bottom padding below my entered text,  an error "[ERROR:flutter/third_party/txt/src/minikin/FontFamily.cpp(184)] Could not get cmap table size!" is logged:
![fedora30](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30557348/107761628-fa94a900-6d2b-11eb-9653-91a0ee1e05a7.png)
- Fedora 31: cursor is visible, no overlap with counter, no padding below my entered text:
![fedora31](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30557348/107761870-5d864000-6d2c-11eb-81c5-8cfa785f38fd.png)
- Centos 7: cursor is visible, no overlap with counter, some additional bottom padding below my entered text:
![centos7](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30557348/107762007-9aeacd80-6d2c-11eb-8e91-d2a29828c30e.png)
May be the the overlap between the text and counter is caused by the logged error "[ERROR:flutter/third_party/txt/src/minikin/FontFamily.cpp(184)] Could not get cmap table size!"?

No that error is a red herring (and it's not even a useful error message).

It's surprising that this would be a distro issue, since Flutter draws its own cursor...

Hi @gspencergoog 
Please look at the attached screen recording taken on Fedora 30 Workstation with Gnome. It`s taken from the code mentioned in HTR above and demonstrates missed cursor. In other distros that I tried the cursor exists (as well as in Android emulator)
https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/30557348/108420157-b590db00-7233-11eb-9726-8d81d22233b1.mp4

-->

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- You must include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

The bug is occurs in the following code:

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
        visualDensity: VisualDensity.adaptivePlatformDensity,
      ),
      home: Scaffold(
        appBar: AppBar(
          title: Text('PageStorageKey bug app'),
        ),
        drawer: Drawer(
          child: ListView(
            physics: BouncingScrollPhysics(),
            key: const PageStorageKey<String>('key_app_drawer'),
            children: <Widget>[

              Container(height: 400, width: 100, color: Colors.red),
              Container(height: 400, width: 100, color: Colors.blue),
              Container(height: 400, width: 100, color: Colors.red),
              Container(height: 400, width: 100, color: Colors.blue),
              Container(height: 400, width: 100, color: Colors.green),

              TextField()

            ],
          ),
        ),
        body: Container(),
      )
    );
  }
}
```

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

The scroll position of the listview should not change when opening and closing the drawer.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->

If the listview in the drawer contains a textfield, after two drawer opens it returns to its top position.

The issue persists on all devices.

I just tried it on stable, beta and dev, no difference.

I think it suggests that this could be related to the particular installation of flutter on my computer, but at this point I have not idea what to look for. Unfortunately I have no other computer to install flutter on.
I notices however, that on a physical phone it sometimes takes more "open/close" actions to reset the scroll position.

I was able to reproduce on `dev` and stable channel, the issue only happens with the following steps:

1. Open the drawer
2. Scroll down
3. Close the drawer
4. Open the drawer (don't scroll the content)
5. Close the drawer
6. Open the drawer and notice that the scroll position was reset.

The issue only happens if there is a TextField at the end of the drawer.

Tested on Android Emulator API 28.

## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Execute `flutter run` on the code sample <!-- (see "Code sample" section below) -->
2. ... Open the text field by clicking on it

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
The label text should have been able to render after the normal render box of the text field and not stop at the outline. 
**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
The label text is cut in half, at the same height as the outline

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(seedColor: Colors.deepPurple),
        useMaterial3: true,
      ),
      home: const MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyHomePage({super.key, required this.title});

  final String title;

  @override
  State<MyHomePage> createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        backgroundColor: Theme.of(context).colorScheme.inversePrimary,
        title: Text(widget.title),
      ),
      body: Align(alignment: Alignment.topCenter, 
        child: Column(
          children: [
            const Text('This is the label text bug'),
            const SizedBox(height: 20),
            Expanded(
              child: PageView(
                scrollDirection: Axis.horizontal,
                children: const [
                  FirstPage(),
                  SecondPage()
                ],
              )
            )
            
          ],
        )
      ),
    );
  }
}

class FirstPage extends StatelessWidget {
  const FirstPage({super.key});
  
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const SingleChildScrollView(child: Column(children: [
      TextField(maxLines: 1, decoration: InputDecoration(border: OutlineInputBorder(), labelText: 'First page'))
    ],));
  }
}

class SecondPage extends StatelessWidget {
  const SecondPage({super.key});
  
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return const SingleChildScrollView(child: Column(children: [
      TextField(maxLines: 1, decoration: InputDecoration(border: OutlineInputBorder(), labelText: 'Second page'))
    ],));
  }
}

comments: Thanks for the report @theskyblockman Looking at the code sample you provided, the behavior seems to be causing due to below: ``` child: PageView( scrollDirection: Axis.horizontal, children: const [ FirstPage(), SecondPage() ], ) ``` ie, wrapping both the classes with `PageView`. If we replace it with `Column`, then the label renders properly as below: ``` Expanded( child: Column( // scrollDirection: Axis.vertical, children: const [ FirstPage(), SecondPage() ], ) ) ``` Also, this behavior is not specific to `material3`, as with `material 2`, same behavior occurs. Do you have any specific use case for which you need to use `PageView` ?
Yes,  I need to use this page view to incorporate a segmented button which chooses a page from this view, so I use the scroll controller to make this task easier

Thanks for the update. The reported behavior is replicable using `PageView`.

issues.title: [Android] Autofill from saved credentials doesn't work
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a form with username and password field
2. Try to use password manager such as Google Password Manager
3. Ensure that password usage requires biometric auth (not sure if it changes anything)
4. Click in Key icon on keyboard and select password

### Device for testing
Google Pixel 7 pro, Android 14 (34)

### Expected results

Form prepopulates with the selected username/password combination

I want to underscore the critical nature of the autofill functionality not working as expected. This issue isn't just about convenience, it directly impacts the first impression our users have and poses significant security concerns:

First Impressions Matter: Autofill is often part of the initial interaction users have with an app. A seamless experience here is crucial for user retention and satisfaction.

Security Implications: A functional "Select strong password" feature is vital for encouraging secure password practices. As of now, while the credentials are saved to the user's password manager, the passwords aren't populated back into the app, leaving users unaware it was ever saved.

Not being able to offer a working sign up process could push users away. Given the importance of both user experience and security, I believe this issue warrants immediate attention and should be labeled a P1. Addressing it would not only align with best practices for application development but also reinforce Flutter's commitment to providing a secure and user-friendly platform.

issues.title: Selecting TextField or TextFormField causes app to freeze for 20-40 seconds on Linux Mint.
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Created a flutter project for linux only using the command `flutter create --platforms=linux textfield_broken`.
2. Copy pasted the code from the "Interactive example" section at the bottom of the page at https://docs.flutter.dev/cookbook/forms/text-input.
3. Run it on Linux Mint 22 Cinnamon.

### Expected results

App to run as shown on the cookbook page and work on every platform whether it be android, linux, windows, etc. i.e., TextField to receive text input and show it on screen and also focusNode to work smoothly.

### Actual results

Code compiled on Linux Mint 22. Worked as expected on web and android phone.
However, on the main machine itself, the app was freezing as soon as any of the TextField or TextFormField was being selected.

Sometimes, after 20-40 seconds, the inputs will be received and displayed on the screen and the app would start to work normally again but unfocusing and clicking on the same or any other TextField or widgets alike would cause the app to freeze for the same amount of time again. FocusNode freezes too and so does the dev tools.

Usually, `{app name} is not responding. Force Quit or Wait` dialogue box pops up. 

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(const MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    const appTitle = 'Form Styling Demo';
    return MaterialApp(
      title: appTitle,
      home: Scaffold(
        appBar: AppBar(
          title: const Text(appTitle),
        ),
        body: const MyCustomForm(),
      ),
    );
  }
}

class MyCustomForm extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyCustomForm({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Column(
      crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
      children: <Widget>[
        const Padding(
          padding: EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 8, vertical: 16),
          child: TextField(
            decoration: InputDecoration(
              border: OutlineInputBorder(),
              hintText: 'Enter a search term',
            ),
          ),
        ),
        Padding(
          padding: const EdgeInsets.symmetric(horizontal: 8, vertical: 16),
          child: TextFormField(
            decoration: const InputDecoration(
              border: UnderlineInputBorder(),
              labelText: 'Enter your username',
            ),
          ),
        ),
      ],
    );
  }
}
```

</details>

Wrapping the text field in an `ExcludeSemantics` did, indeed, fix the problem! Thanks, this means there is a viable workaround for now!

Also, I ran the app with `strace`. The hang occurs on this:
```
poll([{fd=8, events=POLLIN}], 1, 25000) = 0 (Timeout)
```
At that point, nothing happens until the request timeouts after 25 seconds.

FD 8 has been set up with
```
connect(8, {sa_family=AF_UNIX, sun_path="/run/user/1000/at-spi/bus_0"}, 29) = 0
```
and it is used extensively before the problematic point, and also after it.

But, just before the problem occurs, the following can be seen:
```
sendmsg(8, {msg_name=NULL, msg_namelen=0, msg_iov=[{iov_base="l\1\0\1\4\0\0\0\323\0\0\0\177\0\0\0\1\1o\0\34\0\0\0/org/a11"..., iov_len=144}, {iov_base="\0\0\0\0", iov_len=4}], msg_iovlen=2, msg_controllen=0, msg_flags=0}, MSG_NOSIGNAL) = 148
poll([{fd=8, events=POLLIN}], 1, 25000) = 1 ([{fd=8, revents=POLLIN}])
recvmsg(8, {msg_name=NULL, msg_namelen=0, msg_iov=[{iov_base="l\1\0\1\4\0\0\0\323\0\0\0\216\0\0\0\1\1o\0\34\0\0\0/org/a11"..., iov_len=2048}], msg_iovlen=1, msg_controllen=0, msg_flags=MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC}, MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC) = 164
recvmsg(8, {msg_namelen=0}, MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC) = -1 EAGAIN (Resource temporarily unavailable)
poll([{fd=8, events=POLLIN}], 1, 25000) = 0 (Timeout)
```
So, apparently the application sends a message to the socket, expects and receives a response, but then immediately tries to read another response from the socket. This smells like a bug to me - not that I have any idea _where_ the possible bug is :)

```dart

)

@jlahd can you run strace with `-s 65535` so we can see the whole contents of the D-Bus messages sent with `sengmsg`/`recvmsg`? That will help pinpoint it.

Given the above the most likely cause is a broken AT-SPI setup, which is causing calls from ATK to block. This would suggest ATK is unreliable in this setup. Unfortunately our use of ATK doesn't give us any options here, as all the calls are synchronous. The solution is probably:
- Find out what is causing this in Linux Mint and fix it there. It doesn't seem to be affecting other distros, so this is possibly a bug in a package version or setup in Mint.
- Use [D-Bus directly for accessibility in Flutter](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/159460), ensuring we don't have any blocking code like this. This is something I've been thinking about for a while, and is what other toolkits like GTK have done. It would be a lot of work and is a long term solution.

issues.title: Android Back gesture should dismiss text selection menu, not also navigate
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run `flutter create bug`.
2. Update the files by making a SelectableText widget:
```diff
diff --git a/lib/main.dart b/lib/main.dart
index d8a052651..7aca97c4e 100644
--- a/lib/main.dart
+++ b/lib/main.dart
@@ -95,3 +95,3 @@ class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
           children: <Widget>[
-            Text(
+            SelectableText(
               'You have pushed the button this many times:',
```

3. Run the app on Android, and long-press on the text. This will select part of the text and bring up the text selection menu.
4. Activate the system back gesture (i.e., swipe from edge, if on Android 10+ and have that enabled; or tap the system back button otherwise.)

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->

The text selection menu should disappear, and the app should otherwise remain as it is.

This is the normal behavior of the text selection menu on Android, and is a way that users are accustomed to dismissing the menu.

This also parallels the behavior of a popup menu. Flutter's PopupMenuButton correctly implements this behavior: on activating the system back gesture when a popup menu is open, the menu is dismissed and no other navigation happens.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->

The app exits completely.

More generally, the app navigates back, just as if you hadn't had the text selection menu open when you activated the system back gesture. In the sample app, that exits the app.

comments: This issue is somewhat parallel to #72029, which is about the other (that I know of) standard way to dismiss the text selection menu: tap somewhere outside of it.

Wondering if we have a specific "native" behavior about this. I can't find anything on Material Design specs. The Gmail app has the exact same behavior: it navigates back if the selection toolbar is visible and you navigate back (or do a back gesture).
CC @justinmc

I tried this out and I seem to be getting different behavior in different apps.  Is this configurable somehow on Android?

Apps that navigated backwards with the menu up when back was pressed:

  * Gmail
  * Chrome

Apps that dismissed the menu when back was pressed:

  * Google Search App

> Is this configurable somehow on Android?

I haven't found anything related. There is a Material Design specs but it does not mention about this behavior:
https://material.io/design/platform-guidance/android-text-selection-toolbar.html

> I tried this out and I seem to be getting different behavior in different apps.

Interesting -- I hadn't expected this would vary! I tried it in several apps before reporting the issue and they were all consistent. Evidently I didn't try enough of them.

> * Gmail

Yep, I reproduce this behavior -- it navigates back, the same as SelectableText currently does.

> * Chrome

But I don't reproduce here. For me, Chrome's behavior is that it dismisses the menu, the same way I think of as standard. Since we're getting different results, here's more detail to try to help pin down what the variable is:

* I'm on Android 11, in Chrome 89.0.4389.90.
* I'm not aware of any setting about this. One that I guess is at least related is that I've enabled swipe-from-edge as a system Back gesture, replacing the Back button. Seems like that shouldn't affect this but possibly it could.
* I opened up Chrome and went to a page. Then I followed a link to make sure there was a clear thing that navigating back should do.
  * To make this super-concrete, I went to flutter.dev and followed the big blue "Get started" button. But I see the same behavior on a variety of other pages too.
* Then I long-pressed on some text in the page. The text selection menu appeared.
* Then I swiped in from the edge of the screen, making the system Back gesture. The text selection menu disappeared, while the browser remained at the same page.
* To complete the demo, I swiped in again from the edge of the screen, making the same Back gesture. The browser navigated back to the previous page.

> * Google Search App

I see the same behavior as you do: the Back gesture dismisses the menu and doesn't cause a navigation.

Here's a few more:

* Google Maps: Back dismisses the menu and doesn't cause a navigation.
  * Concretely: There isn't a lot of selectable text here, but we can observe this in the search input. Type some text for a search query, then long-press to open the text selection menu. Now the Back gesture dismisses the menu. With the menu gone, the Back gesture dismisses the keyboard. With the keyboard also gone, the Back gesture navigates out of the search screen.
* Wikipedia: Back dismisses the menu and doesn't cause a navigation.
  * This data point is interesting because Wikipedia doesn't use the standard text selection menu -- it appears to have a custom implementation. (I notice this because it lacks the Google Translate menu option.) So their custom implementation has chosen the same behavior.
* Amazon Shopping: Back causes a navigation.
* Facebook Messenger: A third variation -- Back dismisses the menu and also dismisses the keyboard, but doesn't cause a navigation.

So it seems like this behavior isn't as standard as I'd thought. Between that and the absence of anything in Material Design on the question, this looks like a UI choice to be made on the merits.

I do think that dismissing just the menu, and not causing a navigation, is the preferable UI choice even in the absence of a standard.

One reason is consistency: dismissing the menu without causing a navigation is the same thing that Back does when Flutter's PopupMenuButton is open (so in particular for the overflow menu from the app bar), and the same thing as it does to the keyboard when that's open.

The popup menu in particular is a very similar kind of widget to the text selection menu: both of them are small rectangles that appear overlaid on the rest of the visible UI, set off by high elevation. Both offer a handful of menu items in the form of text buttons, and disappear when an item is selected. So I think people will naturally bring expectations from what they've seen of one widget's behavior to their interaction with the other widget, and be confused when it seems to be inconsistent.

The keyboard is less similar, but it's often closely juxtaposed: a frequent use of the text selection menu is inside a text input, when the keyboard is likely to be open. Flutter's current behavior is that when both are open, Back will dismiss the keyboard but leave the text selection menu in place. (I haven't confirmed this in a minimal repro, but it's what I see in a Flutter app I built a few weeks ago.) That feels pretty awkward to me, almost like the menu is stuck. I think it'd be much better to either dismiss the menu and leave the keyboard (like Google Maps and the Google Search app), or dismiss both (like Facebook Messenger).

The other reason is that I think this has an asymmetry of frustration. Given the variation in different apps' behavior, users will come in with conflicting expectations about how this might behave, so unavoidably some will be surprised. But I think there is a much greater potential frustration from having Back go a step further than you were expecting than from going a step less, and so it's better to err on the latter side.

If Back takes a smaller step than you wanted, you hit Back again and you're on your way. But if the whole page gets unexpectedly navigated away when you only wanted to dismiss a menu, then there's potentially all kinds of UI state in that page that gets lost -- scrolling down through a lot of content, expanding pieces of content, even typing into text inputs. Just the first step of navigating forward again can already be nontrivial, depending on how you got to the page in the first place. Losing what you typed into text input boxes has a very high frustration ceiling.

I tried creating a simple native Android app using one of the templates in Android Studio, and the behavior is to dismiss the toolbar first.  That seems to be in agreement with what you're saying @gnprice.



https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/389558/112393273-0b83f300-8cb8-11eb-85f3-04d85a565574.mov

~~Another way this manifests is during text input. When the Flutter navigation stack is only 1 page deep, focus on a text field and open the soft keyboard.~~

~~It seems like the current system back handling is a bit too simplistic.~~

https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/a921c1cf3d2616704bcbb83ab13d89e39be0c97c/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/binding.dart#L637

~~The expected behavior should be that the soft keyboard is dismissed and we remain on the same page. The actual behavior is that it leaves the app (or pops the Flutter activity/viewcontroller in add-to-app).~~

Update: sorry for the noise, the issue isn't in the framework

import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

class Test extends StatefulWidget {
  static const routeName = 'test';

  @override
  _TestState createState() => _TestState();
}

class _TestState extends State<Test> {
  FocusNode _textFocus;

  @override
  void dispose() {
    _textFocus.dispose();
    super.dispose();
  }

  @override
  void initState() {
    _textFocus = FocusNode();
    super.initState();
  }
  final firstFocus = FocusNode(onKey: (FocusNode focusNode, RawKeyEvent event) {
    print('first');
    return false;
  });

  final secondFocus =
      FocusNode(onKey: (FocusNode focusNode, RawKeyEvent event) {
    print('second');
    return false;
  });

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    _textFocus.requestFocus();

    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: Text('PlutoGrid'),
      ),
      body: RawKeyboardListener(
        focusNode: firstFocus,
        child: Container(
          width: 100,
          height: 80,
          child: RawKeyboardListener(
            focusNode: secondFocus,
            child: TextField(
              focusNode: _textFocus,
              // textInputAction: TextInputAction.none,
              readOnly: true,
              onTap: () {
                print('tap');
              },
              onSubmitted: (String value) {
                print('submitted');
              },
            ),
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
``` 
	
</details>

first
second

It looks like the issue is caused by the following behaviour of the flutter engine : 

https://github.com/flutter/engine/blob/cbda68a720904137ee9dfdf840db323afcf52705/lib/web_ui/lib/src/engine/text_editing/text_editing.dart#L1459

The KeyEvent for the enter key is prevented from bubbling up further and will not be reported to the FocusNode. The explanation given in the comment for this behaviour is that the engine wants to prevent adding a newline at the end of the input for non multiline TexFields. I wonder if this is actually required, as non multiline TextFields are handled with regular input html elements, which should not add a new line anyway.
Hmm.. if I am facing the same issue, this is not the reason, since I am using a multi-line text field.
